_note: IG carousel config for pb-banana-biscuits. Each slide has a fully-detailed cinematic prompt for Nano Banana Pro + deterministic text overlay rendered on top via PIL.

slides.prompt: Cinematic food photography, slightly off-axis overhead angle of a generous pile of freshly-baked bone-shaped peanut butter dog biscuits tumbling across a weathered oak cutting board. Single biscuit tipped off the edge catching the light mid-fall. Deep amber side-light from the right casting long dramatic shadows to the left. Rich golden-brown tops with visible cracks and texture. A few scattered oats glowing in the warm light. Dark negative space in the upper left. Shot on 50mm at f/2.8, warm tungsten highlights, shallow depth of field. Home kitchen — not studio. Magazine-quality but honest and imperfect. No text, no labels, no watermarks, no logos, no people, no dogs, no cutlery, no spoons, no forks, no knives, no packaging.

Cinematic overhead flat-lay food photography with dramatic side-light from the upper right casting long warm shadows across a weathered oak surface: a small cream ceramic bowl of whole wheat flour with a little flour spilled onto the wood, a small wooden dish of rolled oats, one perfectly ripe banana with speckled peel, a plain unmarked glass jar of natural peanut butter — peanut butter glistening at the rim, unmarked metal lid off to one side, a single whole brown egg resting in its shell, a small glass measuring cup with a shallow pool of water. Generous negative space between items. Warm tungsten-toned light, deep shadows. Absolutely no text on anything, no writing, no watermarks, no logos, no jars with labels, no utensils, no cutlery, no hands, no people, no dogs.

Cinematic close-up food photography at an eye-level 30-degree angle: a flour-dusted wooden rolling pin resting mid-roll across pale tan biscuit dough on a rustic oak counter. A bone-shaped cookie cutter pressed into the dough in the foreground, and one freshly-cut bone shape lifted and set aside. A small puff of flour suspended in mid-air above the dough, catching warm side-light. Deep shadows to the left, glow from the right. Shallow depth of field — the cutter sharp, the rolling pin softly blurred. Honest home-kitchen feel, no studio gloss. No text, no labels, no logos, no hands, no people, no dogs.

Cinematic close-up food photography at an overhead 45-degree angle of freshly baked BONE-SHAPED peanut butter dog biscuits cooling on a black wire rack. Every biscuit is cut into the shape of a classic dog bone — two round lobes on each end connected by a narrower middle. Thin, flat biscuits (about 1/4 inch thick), NOT puffy, NOT round, NOT scones. Tops are deeply golden-brown with visibly crackled texture and a slight glossiness catching warm side-light from the right. The rack wires cast long dramatic parallel shadows across the weathered wooden counter below. Tight crop — 3 or 4 bone-shaped biscuits in sharp focus up front, more softly out of focus in the background. A few peanut-colored crumbs scattered on the counter. One bone tilted up on its edge, catching the light. Honest imperfect plating, home kitchen feel, magazine-quality. No text, no labels, no watermarks, no logos, no hands, no people, no dogs, no cutlery.
